metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC to e1f2 (or a dedicated micro-task) that requires posting the three-part measurement-framework comment to the epic: (a) the 0 finding-blocks/162-changes baseline, (b) the re-runnable REVIEW_RESULT sidecar queries and norm_id survival commands, and (c) the link to ticket 72b6.
findings.finding: Parent AC2 (the in-session measurement-framework artifact recorded as an epic comment — baseline numbers, re-runnable queries, and a link to the deferred follow-up ticket) is not owned as an acceptance criterion by any child. e1f2's What prose mentions recording the observation plan on the epic, but its AC checklist does not include this as a verifiable item, so the completion verifier cannot confirm it was done.
findings.location: Parent AC2; no child AC covers it — e1f2 What prose gestures at it but e1f2's AC checklist omits it
findings.suggested_fix: Add to e1f2's AC checklist: '- [ ] A comment is posted on this epic recording (a) the 2026-07-12 baseline (0 finding-blocks / 162 changes; ~13 advisories/round), (b) the exact re-runnable queries for code-review block/false-block counts (REVIEW_RESULT code_review_result_v1 sidecars) and plan-review advisory-uptake (norm_id survival across rounds), and (c) the link to the deferred follow-up ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d.' Alternatively, create a dedicated child task scoped to writing the epic measurement-framework comment.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rewrite b9c0 AC4 to be conditional on the derived threshold, e.g.: 'A replay shows findings at impact >= block_threshold now BLOCK; the 0.54-band (#518-class) and 0.60-band (#539-class) findings are each labeled as blocking or non-blocking consistent with the derived threshold, with the rationale documented.'
findings.finding: b9c0's Acceptance Criterion 4 ('A replay of the post-recal corpus shows #518-class and #539-class findings now BLOCK') is structurally contingent on 9f25 deriving a threshold at or below 0.54 (for #518) and 0.60 (for #539). If 9f25's precision analysis yields a threshold above 0.54, the #518-class findings (impact 0.54) would not block, causing b9c0's own AC4 to fail — but b9c0 has no fallback or conditional framing for this scenario.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace b9c0 AC4 with: '- [ ] A replay of the post-recal corpus shows that findings with impact >= the derived block_threshold now BLOCK; the replay explicitly documents whether #518-class (impact 0.54) and #539-class (impact 0.60) findings fall above or below the threshold, consistent with 9f25's derivation.'

findings.finding: Child ticket d4cf-0780-cbed-4879 ('Plan-review criterion: leaf-plan parent-containment') appears in the session log's ticket graph but is silently absent from the plan — not mentioned in the body, success criteria, acceptance criteria, or definitions section.
Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add a parent success criterion / AC covering the leaf-plan parent-containment outcome that d4cf delivers, or move d4cf out of this epic into a separate one where it is properly anchored.
findings.finding: Child d4cf (leaf-plan parent-containment G7 criterion) is not anchored to any parent success criterion or acceptance criterion — it adds new scope to the epic that the parent never describes as a deliverable. This is a scope-expansion that the parent's completion verifier will require to be closed (via AC1 'all child tickets closed') without the parent's success criteria ever specifying the outcome.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the parent's Success Criteria: 'A plan-review criterion (G7) checks that leaf ticket scope is contained within its parent's declared scope, ships advisory at 0.95, and has observable fire-rate in REVIEW_RESULT sidecars.' Alternatively, if this is truly out-of-scope for this calibration epic, extract d4cf into its own epic or a follow-up.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either remove `flattering-ethologic-cassowary` from this epic and track it under a dedicated plan-review improvements initiative, or add an explicit Success Criterion and AC that derives the leaf-plan parent-containment rule from the 2026-07-12 calibration findings.
findings.finding: Child `flattering-ethologic-cassowary` (leaf-plan parent-containment criterion) introduces an independent new plan-review criterion that is not grounded in any of the three stated calibration problems (Context items 1–3), not covered by any Success Criterion, and not referenced in any AC — making it a bundled heterogeneous concern that belongs in a separate initiative rather than this calibration epic.
findings.location: Decomposition State — child: flattering-ethologic-cassowary; Context section (problems 1–3); Success Criteria section

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign ownership of creating ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d (idea, discovered_from this epic, [operator-attested] criterion) to an existing child (e.g., e1f2 or 9f25) or add a dedicated micro-task.
findings.finding: Parent AC3 (file the deferred post-deployment measurement as a separate follow-up ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d, linked `discovered_from` this epic, parked in `idea`, with `[operator-attested]` data-collection criterion) has no owning child AC.
findings.location: Parent AC3; no child in the roster covers it
findings.suggested_fix: Add to e1f2's AC checklist: '- [ ] Ticket 72b6-b0d2-2f93-456d exists in `idea` status, linked `discovered_from` this epic, with a data-collection criterion marked `[operator-attested]`.' Alternatively, attach it to 9f25 since that child produces the measurement corpus that motivates the follow-up.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a coordination note in ac0e and/or e1f2 acknowledging the shared CLAUDE.md edit, or declare an ordering dependency (e.g., ac0e depends_on e1f2 or vice versa) so they are serialized.
findings.finding: Children ac0e and e1f2 both edit CLAUDE.md with no declared ordering or coordination note between them, creating an implicit shared-state risk for merge conflicts or unintended cross-section interference.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `depends_on: e1f2-0bcc-dbe7-4750` to ac0e (or vice versa), ensuring they land sequentially. Alternatively, add a note in each child's What: 'Coordinate with sibling [X] which also edits CLAUDE.md; apply both changes in a single commit or verify the other section is intact.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name the specific artifact where the signed-children anti-pattern must be documented (e.g., 'added as a named section in `docs/completion-verifier.md`' or 'a new ADR entry'), so the criterion is deterministically verifiable in-session.
findings.finding: 'The signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented' names no artifact — an executor cannot verify this criterion without knowing where the documentation must land (ADR, CLAUDE.md, code comment, wiki page, etc.).
findings.location: Success Criteria — third bullet: 'the signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented'
findings.suggested_fix: Change the success criterion to: 'the signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented in `docs/completion-verifier.md` (or equivalent canonical location) as a named anti-pattern with a one-paragraph explanation and a pointer to bug 02a3.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in SC3 (and add an AC if needed) whether the certifiable:false escape hatch is to be restored/protected as part of this epic or explicitly deferred — the current text only documents the anti-pattern, leaving the described structural defect uncovered by any fix criterion.
findings.finding: Context explicitly names 'children closed (signed) epic ACs defeat the certifiable:false escape hatch (bf50/5f39 contagion, bug 02a3)' as a structural defect to fix, but SC3 only requires that 'the signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented' — no Success Criterion or AC verifies that the certifiable:false escape hatch mechanism is restored or protected. The described structural problem (broken escape hatch) has no covering fix criterion; only its documentation is required.
findings.location: Context item (3) vs Success Criteria bullet 3 vs AC1
findings.suggested_fix: Either add a sub-bullet to SC3: 'The certifiable:false escape hatch is verified to function correctly after the documented anti-pattern is addressed' and a matching AC; OR add a sentence to SC3 explicitly scoping escape-hatch restoration out of this epic and deferring it to a named follow-up ticket.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] In AC2, define 'norm_id' (or add a pointer to where it is defined) and clarify what 'survival across rounds' means operationally — e.g. the schema/table it lives in and the query shape — so the re-runnable measurement criterion is self-contained.
findings.finding: AC2 introduces the term 'norm_id survival across rounds' as a re-runnable measurement query without any prior definition or explanation of what 'norm_id' refers to in the plan body. This makes the AC's measurement step unverifiable for anyone without out-of-band context.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC2 bullet — 'plan-review advisory-uptake via norm_id survival across rounds'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a parenthetical such as '(`norm_id` = the stable finding identifier in the `PLAN_REVIEW_FINDING` sidecar; survival = the finding appears in round N+1 unremediated)' or a pointer to the schema definition.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out plan for the blocking threshold (e.g., a named feature flag, a one-line config revert procedure, or an emergency-disable runbook step) so that if false-positive blocking spikes post-deployment there is a documented, tested path to disable blocking without a full review cycle.
findings.finding: The plan enables a production blocking threshold (BLOCK verdicts for serious-tier findings) contingent on passing the ADR-0036 A/B gate, but provides no explicit rollback or back-out path if false-positive blocking spikes after the threshold is live. The A/B gate is a pre-flight check, not a rollback mechanism — once flipped, there is no stated procedure to disable blocking without a new change going through its own review cycle.
findings.location: Success Criteria (first bullet) and Definitions & Sequencing (b9c0 / code-v2 blocking enablement)
findings.suggested_fix: In the Definitions & Sequencing section, add a subsection: 'Rollback: blocking is gated by a single boolean in `decide.py` (or equivalent config entry); to disable, flip it to False and land as a hotfix — this does not require ADR-0036 re-approval and is pre-authorized for on-call use.' Reference the flag name in b9c0's ACs so it is verifiable.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC line that names a specific, verifiable artifact proving advisory remediation is no longer mandated (e.g., 'CLAUDE.md diff removing mandatory-remediation language is landed and linked here').
findings.finding: The advisory-guidance loosening goal has no end-state-observable proof in the Acceptance Criteria. Success Criteria says 'Agent guidance no longer MANDATES advisory remediation' but no AC line specifies a verifiable artifact (e.g., a CLAUDE.md diff, a named doc update, or a configuration flag) that can be checked to confirm the mandate was removed.
findings.location: Success Criteria (advisory-guidance loosening bullet) and Acceptance Criteria section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] The guidance document (CLAUDE.md or equivalent) no longer contains language mandating remediation of all valid advisories; the specific section/line removed is cited in the merge comment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a child ticket (or extend an existing one) that specifies and implements the FAIL-verdict persistence mechanism — state explicitly where the verdict is written (e.g., appended as a `COMPLETION_FAIL` ticket event, or a comment on the ticket), so the 'durably persisted' AC is verifiable in-session.
findings.finding: The Success Criterion for workstream 3 states 'FAIL verdicts are durably persisted' but no child ticket and no plan section specifies the persistence mechanism — currently transition_close raises CommandError and the FAIL verdict is never written to the ticket store. It is unclear whether 'durable persistence' means appending a ticket event, writing a comment, or something else, and no child ticket implements this.
findings.location: Success Criteria (third bullet) — 'Completion-verifier FAILs are durably persisted and enumerate all unmet criteria in one verdict'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the 'piecemeal ratchet' root cause is (a) agent early-termination (prompt/behavior fix needed) or (b) a verdict-shape/storage issue (code-path fix needed), and add a corresponding child ticket targeting the correct layer.
findings.finding: The 'FAILs ratchet piecemeal' problem (cited in Context as the 3b4e incident requiring 2 extra CI cycles) is attributed to a structural code defect, but the completion verifier's `reconcile_verdict` already returns ALL findings in a single verdict — the piecemeal behavior is more likely an agent behavior issue (the agent terminates early after finding the first failing criterion). The plan provides no positive rationale for why the chosen fix (implied: code/storage path change) solves a problem that may actually be in the prompt/agent behavior.
findings.location: Context section — 'FAILs ratchet piecemeal (3b4e took 2 extra CI cycles)'; Success Criteria — 'enumerate all unmet criteria in one verdict'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether the `security` criterion joins serious-tier via (a) setting `blocking_enabled:true` in `criteria_routing.json` independently of the consequence-binary model, or (b) being excluded from b9c0's enabled set (since it has no consequence binary and no field-calibrated threshold); the plan currently gives b9c0 an inconsistent specification.
findings.finding: The 'serious-tier' definition in Definitions & Sequencing conflates two structurally distinct mechanisms — consequence binaries in decide.py (_CODE_PROD_BINARIES/_CODE_MAINT_BINARIES at _CODE_TIER_SERIOUS=0.9) and the 'security' overlay criterion in criteria_routing.json (AGENT exec, blocking_enabled:false, no consequence binary) — without specifying the processing protocol that would enable 'security' under the same serious-tier blocking path as the other five binaries.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback rule for the A/B gate failure case in b9c0's specification (e.g., 'if the A/B gate exits non-zero, b9c0 ships with blocking_enabled remaining false and re-queues after a minimum corpus size N is reached').
findings.finding: The plan specifies no fallback or decision rule for what b9c0 does if the ADR-0036 A/B gate fails (i.e., the labeled-set separation test does not pass). The plan says the flip happens 'before flipping' but does not state whether b9c0 blocks, defers, or ships permissive if the gate exits non-zero.
findings.location: Success Criteria: 'passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.` to 74d9's AC checklist for consistency with sibling gate pattern.
findings.finding: Child 74d9 omits a `make typecheck` AC, inconsistent with siblings 24ec, b9c0, and f32e that all include typecheck. While 74d9's change is prompt-file-only (.md), the AC pattern across siblings establishes typecheck as a standard gate, and if any Python glue is touched (e.g., completion_verifier type stubs or result parsing), the gap becomes material.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to 74d9's Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] make typecheck passes on the change.'

findings.finding: AC2 ('A follow-up measurement note records post-change field data: code-review block/false-block counts, and plan-review advisory-uptake behavior after the guidance loosening') is explicitly post-sprint — it requires observing multi-day field behavior after deployment and cannot be evaluated in-session.
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC2 into: (a) an in-session AC — 'A follow-up tracking ticket is opened with the measurement template (metrics: block count, false-block count, advisory uptake %) and a stated review date of T+30 days'; (b) a deferred note — 'Field data will be recorded in that ticket; it is not a gate on this epic's closure.'

findings.finding: AC2 of the parent ('a follow-up measurement note on the epic records post-change field data: code-review block/false-block counts and advisory-uptake behavior') is not owned as an acceptance criterion by any child. e1f2 mentions recording an observation plan in its What section, but its ACs do not commit to posting the measurement comment on the epic. b9c0's replay AC is pre-merge, not post-go-live field data. No child AC requires the durable post-change field measurement note that completes the parent's AC2.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to e1f2's ACs: '- [ ] A comment is posted on the parent epic with post-change advisory-uptake data (norm_id survival rate before/after) measured from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars after the guidance change is live.' Add to b9c0's ACs: '- [ ] A comment is posted on the parent epic with post-go-live code-review block/false-block counts measured from the first N changes after the threshold flip.'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
